metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning about views, grids, and maps

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Using position words (beside, between, in front of, behind)

questions.question: If you are facing your teacher, which hand do you write with? (For most people) That is your:
questions.explanation: Most people write with their right hand. Knowing left and right helps us describe where things are and give directions!

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice with the L-shape trick: your left hand makes an L

questions.question: A bird is flying in the sky. The bird is _______ you.
questions.explanation: A bird flying in the sky is above you because it is higher up. 'Above' means something is at a higher position than you.

questions.question: Your shoes are on the floor. The floor is _______ the table.
questions.explanation: The floor is below the table because it is at a lower position. 'Below' means something is lower down.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Difference between vertical and horizontal positions
commonMistakes.remediation: Use real objects to show above/below vs beside

questions.question: There are three children standing in a line: Anu, Balu, and Chitra. Balu is in the middle. Balu is _______ Anu and Chitra.
questions.explanation: 'Between' means in the middle of two things. Balu is standing between Anu and Chitra, with one on each side.

questions.question: A cat is sitting next to a box, on the same side. The cat is _______ the box.
questions.explanation: 'Beside' means next to something, on the same level. The cat is sitting beside the box, right next to it.

questions.question: Which shape has NO corners at all?
questions.explanation: A circle is round and has no corners (vertices) at all. Squares have 4 corners, triangles have 3, and rectangles have 4.

questions.question: A ball is a 3D shape. What is the name of this shape?
questions.explanation: A ball is shaped like a sphere. A sphere is perfectly round from every direction, like a football or a marble.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Distinguishing between round and box-like 3D shapes
commonMistakes.remediation: Handle real objects: a ball (sphere) vs a dice (cube)

questions.question: If you look at a tin can (cylinder) from the top, what shape do you see?
questions.explanation: When you look at a tin can from the top, you see its round opening, which is a circle! From the side, it would look like a rectangle.

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused top view with side view of cylinder
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding that different views show different shapes
commonMistakes.remediation: Look at real cans from different angles

questions.question: Imagine you are standing in front of your school. Your friend is standing behind the school. Can your friend see you?
options: No, the building blocks the view
questions.correctAnswerText: No, the building blocks the view
questions.explanation: If a big building is between you and your friend, you cannot see each other because the building blocks the view. Objects can hide other objects!

questions.question: In a classroom, there are 3 rows and 4 columns of desks. How many desks are there in total?
questions.explanation: If there are 3 rows with 4 desks in each row, there are 3 x 4 = 12 desks in total. Rows go across and columns go up and down!

commonMistakes.mistake: Added rows and columns instead of multiplying
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding rows and columns form a grid
commonMistakes.remediation: Draw a grid and count objects in rows and columns

excellent.message: Wonderful! You know your directions and shapes really well. You are ready to learn about views, grids, and maps!

good.message: Great job! You understand positions and shapes nicely. Let's explore how objects look from different sides!

partial.message: You know some things well, but let's review directions and shapes a bit more. Watch the review videos first, then we will start the fun chapter!

needs_review.message: Let's first practice left-right, above-below, and shape names. We have some fun videos to help you get ready for this exciting chapter!

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness check for Hide and Seek!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: No worries! We have some really fun videos about directions and shapes to help you get ready.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Awesome! You are all set to discover how objects look from different views and play grid games!
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can try this quiz again to see how much you have improved.
